response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with a craftsmanship mindset, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. He lists no SQL Server or Oracle experience (MySQL/MongoDB instead), and has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets exposure — RIDE Capital was retail-investment fintech built on Node/GraphQL, not investment banking systems. The desirable extras he does have (CI/CD, scripting, some Java) cannot offset the missing core requirements.
strengths: Proven low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O and caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored open-source testing tools
Polyglot adaptability across systems, web, and embedded, with solid communication skills (workshops, conference talks)
concerns: Professional C++ experience is roughly seven months and over a decade old, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets/investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle background; database experience is MySQL and MongoDB

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain
